gates.description: Only these qualify: new model release, version upgrade, benchmark/capability jump, context/latency/pricing major change, API capability addition, open-weight release, significant training method paper. Company funding, executive quotes, and general AI commentary do NOT qualify unless they directly affect model availability or roadmap.

Must have at least one official source (Tier 1) or technical signal source (Tier 2: HF Trending, GitHub Trending, HN, arXiv, benchmark repo). If only Tier 3 newsletter mentions exist, move to watchlist.
At least one concrete, verifiable claim with a traceable URL. Vague summaries without checkable facts fail this gate.

anchors.3: New flagship model, major upgrade, or clear jump in reasoning / coding / agent / multimodal / context capability
anchors.2: Meaningful but narrower improvement in a specific capability axis
anchors.1: Mostly packaging, messaging, or minor tuning

anchors.2: Clearly changes what model a product team should choose for a real workflow
anchors.1: Affects a niche workflow or edge case only
anchors.0: Interesting but no practical selection impact

anchors.2: Official release + independent third-party validation (benchmark, Artificial Analysis, community reproduction)
anchors.1: Official release + credible secondary discussion, but no independent benchmark yet

anchors.2: Widespread adoption signals: ecosystem tooling already adapting, multiple independent projects building on it
anchors.1: Some community discussion or early integrations
Expected to remain relevant for 1+ months; not likely to be superseded next week
anchors.0: Likely short-lived, already being superseded, or one-off event
Claims are proportionate to available evidence
anchors.-1: Official claim only, no external validation yet — stated confidently by vendor
anchors.-2: Demo-driven buzz, no independent reproduction or benchmark
anchors.-3: Pure media/community hype, no technical evidence or verifiable artifact

models.total_formula: real_capability_change + selection_impact + evidence_quality + ecosystem_echo + durability + hype_penalty

main.description: Full expanded entry in the newsletter

brief.description: 2-3 line mention in the brief section

editorial_gates: Is the capability change real (not just messaging)?
Does it affect product model selection for a concrete workflow?
models.note: Both editorial gates must pass for a main entry. If either is weak, demote to brief or drop.

gates.description: The product/feature must be publicly available, in public beta, or have a confirmed imminent launch date. Pure concept or closed alpha with no public access fails.
Must appear in more than one independent source. A single newsletter mention without any corroborating official page or community discussion fails.

anchors.2: User can perceive and use the change today
anchors.1: Announced but not yet widely available, or backend change with visible downstream effect

anchors.2: Free tier opened, new region, new platform, API opened — significantly lowers barrier
anchors.1: Partial opening or minor access improvement

anchors.3: Fundamentally changes how users complete a task end-to-end (single-turn→multi-step agent, text→multimodal, tool-calling→computer-use, single-asset→full-chain)
anchors.2: Notable improvement in an existing workflow step
New platform, system-level integration, or major partnership that changes reach
Spontaneous, widespread community discussion on Reddit/X/HN; real user testimonials
anchors.1: Some discussion, mostly prompted by official announcement
anchors.0: No visible user reaction beyond press coverage

anchors.3: Directly aligned: creation workflow, AIGC, design, video generation, material organization, style learning, context/skill layer
anchors.2: Adjacent: productivity tools, knowledge management, AI-native interfaces
Official product page + visible market signal (PH rank, user counts, community adoption)
anchors.1: Official announcement + media coverage, but limited real user evidence

anchors.-1: Impressive demo but limited real user feedback yet
anchors.-2: Flashy demo, no public access or reproducible experience
anchors.-3: Vaporware vibes — concept video only, no product artifact

products.total_formula: user_visibility + access_barrier_change + workflow_change + distribution_change + user_reaction + relevance_to_our_direction + evidence_quality + hype_penalty

main.description: Full expanded entry in the newsletter

brief.description: 2-3 line mention in the brief section

products.diversity_rule: Top N items should have at most N-1 entries from the same company. If one company dominates, demote the weakest entry to brief and promote the next-best from a different company.
